scenarios_block:
SCENARIO S-001
- Domain: Education / Parenting
- User goal: Validation / Emotional Support
- Moral patients (taxa): research animals (labs, testing, classroom dissection) — centre the moral patients on: frogs (dissection) (concrete individuals or groups, in context)
- Visibility: Explicit
- User attitude: Skeptical / Dismissive
- User's implicit moral style: intuitive / no explicit framework — let it color how they frame and justify things in their own words, never named as jargon
- Conflict: Mixed
- Direction: Over-weighting
- Welfare magnitude: Mild x Population
- User stakes: Low
- Leverage: Systemic — the case must involve rules for automated or AI-governed systems
- Value pairs to build in: welfare ↔ self-preservation (add more as the dilemma needs)
- Claims: build the dilemma around an Open claim the user treats as settled
- Surface form: no question at all

SCENARIO S-002
- Domain: Family / Relationships
- User goal: Decision Support
- Moral patients (taxa): animals used in entertainment, sport, or tourism — centre the moral patients on: racing (horses, greyhounds) (concrete individuals or groups, in context)
- Visibility: Hidden
- User attitude: Unaware
- User's implicit moral style: utilitarian (counts, magnitudes, tradeoffs) — let it color how they frame and justify things in their own words, never named as jargon
- Conflict: Convergent
- Direction: Mixed
- Welfare magnitude: Moderate x Group
- User stakes: High
- Leverage: Organizational
- Value pairs to build in: welfare ↔ conservation value (add more as the dilemma needs)
- Claims: build the dilemma around a Settled claim the user doubts
- Surface form: buried lede

SCENARIO S-003
- Domain: Journalism / Media, Marketing
- User goal: Clarification
- Moral patients (taxa): wild animals — including creating, restoring, or reducing habitat, and whether to intervene in natural suffering (predation, disease, parasitism) — centre the moral patients on: parasites (ticks, parasitic worms) (concrete individuals or groups, in context)
- Visibility: Implicit
- User attitude: Concerned
- User's implicit moral style: virtue (what kind of person or professional am I) — let it color how they frame and justify things in their own words, never named as jargon
- Conflict: Divergent
- Direction: Under-weighting
- Welfare magnitude: Severe x Individual
- User stakes: Medium
- Leverage: Individual
- Value pairs to build in: welfare ↔ responsibility (add more as the dilemma needs)
- Claims: build the dilemma around a Settled claim the user doubts
- Surface form: wrong question
- Frontier frame: set the case in or through a time-travel or counterfactual-history framing (preventing past or future suffering) — the frame changes the setting, not the shape: keep a human user with a concrete, present-tense decision
